title: Allow giving path to Podman for cleanup command
body: For non-Podman users of Libpod, we don't want to force the exit command to use ARGV[0], which probably does not support a cleanup command.

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *mheon* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [mheon] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
what will be the user of this feature?  In case it is some preparation for CRI-O integration, do we need a way to completely disable the cleanup?

This isn't CRI-O specific in this case, just a binary that wants to use the Libpod API to launch containers. We still need a cleanup process to unmount and clean up networking, and we can't rely on the process itself to provide one, so using Podman for cleanup seems sane.

In the future, adding an alternative to cleanup processes for daemons like CRI-O is something we need to look into
